items.title: Robin Hanson on the Future of Large Language Models | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Robots may one day rule the world, but what is a robot-ruled Earth like? Dr. Robin Hanson explores the implications of a future world in which researchers have not created artificial general intelligence but have learned to copy humans onto computers, creating "ems," or emulated people, who quickly come to outnumber the real ones.

Robin Hanson is an Associate Professor of Economics, and received his Ph.D in social sciences from Caltech. He joined George Mason's economics faculty in 1999 after completing a two-year post-doc at U.C Berkely. His major fields of interest include health policy, regulation, and formal political theory.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

This Chat was made possible by MindFest 2025: "Sentience, Autonomy, and the Future of Human-AI Interaction", a two-day event presented by the Center for the Future Mind.

To learn more about groundbreaking innovation in AI, neuroscience, and the study of consciousness, visit the Center for the Future Mind, view MindFest videos, and subscribe to their newsletter, visit: https://shorturl.at/mYtqz.</article>

items.title: Joshua Swamidass - Sex, Sexuality, and Gender
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Show your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase, from hoodies and tees to mugs and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Sexual questions abound. What is “normal” sexual behavior vs. multiple variations? How to appreciate LGBTQ+ issues? What can be learned from sex in animal models that is relevant for human sexuality? A philosophical perspective can provide clarity.

Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

S. Joshua Swamidass is an American computational biologist, physician, academic, and author. He is an associate professor of Laboratory and Genomic Medicine, and a Faculty Lead of Translational Bioinformatics in the Institute for Informatics at Washington University in St. Louis.

items.title: Holmes Rolston - Science vs. Religion
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Science and religion—each pursues large truths and offers total solutions. Science works; that’s for sure. Religion? Not so sure. What are the thought processes, the ways of thinking, of science and religion? How do the ‘scientific method’ and the ‘religious method’ compare and contrast? Moreover, how to develop trust in the separate truths of science and religion?

Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Holmes Rolston III was an American philosopher who was University Distinguished Professor of Philosophy at Colorado State University. He is best known for his contributions to environmental ethics and the relationship between science and religion.

items.title: Stacie Friend - What are the Aspects of Consciousness?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

We offer distinctive approaches to consciousness including cognitive science, phenomenology, and even the implications of aesthetics like fiction and films. We observe the richness of consciousness studies.

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Stacie Friend is a Reader in Philosophy at the University of Edinburgh. She previously taught at Birkbeck and Heythrop College, University of London. Her research is at the intersection of aesthetics, language and mind, especially as these pertain to our engagement with works of fiction.

items.title: Elliott Sober - Philosophy of Evolutionary Biology
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Watch more videos on this topic: https://shorturl.at/dtvq9

How are the terms “function,” “fitness,” and “adaptation” used in the context of evolution? How does function relate to fitness and thus to reproductive success? How do adaptations occur and which ones survive and prosper?

Donate what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Elliott R. Sober is an American philosopher. He is noted for his work in philosophy of biology and general philosophy of science. Sober is Hans Reichenbach Professor and William F. Vilas Research Professor Emeritus in the Department of Philosophy at the University of Wisconsin–Madison.

items.title: Frank Tipler - Will the Universe Ever End?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>For subscriber-only exclusives, register for a free membership today: https://closertotruth.com/register/

What does it mean to ask about the end of the universe? Can the universe even have an end? What would end? In the far, far future, what happens to stars, galaxies, and black holes? What about mass and energy, even space and time? What’s the ‘Big Crunch’ and the ‘Big Rip’? And what if there are multiple universes, will the multiverse ever end?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on any platform for new episodes each Wednesday: https://shorturl.at/BCxtj

Frank Jennings Tipler is an American mathematical physicist and cosmologist, holding a joint appointment in the Departments of Mathematics and Physics at Tulane University. 

For subscriber-only exclusives, register for a free membership today: https://closertotruth.com/register/

items.title: Chris French on the Science of Weird Shit | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Ghostly encounters, alien abductions, reincarnation, talking to the dead, UFO sightings, inexplicable coincidences, out-of-body and near-death experiences. Are these legitimate phenomena? If not, how should we go about understanding them? In his latest book, Chris French investigates paranormal claims to discover what lurks behind this “weird shit”. French provides authoritative, evidence-based explanations for a wide range of superficially mysterious phenomena, and then goes further to draw out lessons with wider applications to many other aspects of modern society where critical thinking is urgently needed.

Chris French's book, "The Science of Weird Shit: Why Our Minds Conjure the Paranormal", is available for purchase now: https://amzn.to/43rxMoQ

Chris French is Emeritus Professor and Head of the Anomalistic Psychology Research Unit in the Psychology Department at Goldsmiths, University of London. He is a Fellow of the British Psychological Society and of the Committee for Skeptical Inquiry and a Patron of Humanists UK. He is the coauthor of Anomalistic Psychology: Exploring Paranormal Belief and Experience.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e</article>

items.title: Psychedelics: Questioning Reality and the Nature of Experience
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>Altered states of consciousness, such as meditation or drug-induced experiences, radically alter one’s consciousness. But do these altered states provide evidence about the nature of consciousness? Bernardo Kastrup explores. #shorts</article>

items.title: Eleonore Stump - Can the Bible Address Philosophical Questions?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Philosophy of religion poses numerous questions about God: “proof” or “disproofs” of existence, nature and attributes, personhood, relation to time, relation to humankind — it’s a long list. In Judeo-Christian religions, the ultimate authority is supposed to be the Bible. But the Bible seems radically different from philosophy of religion.

Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Eleonore Stump is an American philosopher and the Robert J. Henle Professor of Philosophy at Saint Louis University, where she has taught since 1992.

items.title: Neil Gillman - What is Eternal Life Like?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Instagram for updates, announcements, and new videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

How to imagine the experience of eternal life? Would we sense ourselves? How would we feel? Whom would we know? What would we do? What would God do? Living forever seems so absurd, yet eternal life is the promise of almost every religion. But if we cannot even imagine what eternal life will be, how can we have hope in its reality?

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Neil Gillman was a Canadian-American rabbi and philosopher affiliated with Conservative Judaism.

items.title: Anil Seth - Theories of Consciousness
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Register for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

Explore diverse theories of consciousness including materialism, dualism, panpsychism, idealism, and diverse ways of thinking about consciousness from the perspectives of neuroscience, philosophy, wisdom traditions. Compare and contrast diverse theories of consciousness as they are arrayed on a “Landscape of Consciousness”.

Contribute what you can to support the show and help Closer To Truth continue exploring life's deepest questions: shorturl.at/OnyRq

Anil Kumar Seth is a British neuroscientist and professor of Cognitive and Computational Neuroscience at the University of Sussex. A proponent of materialist explanations of consciousness, he is currently amongst the most cited scholars on the topics of neuroscience and cognitive science globally.

items.content:
<article>Show your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase, from hoodies and tees to mugs and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

The nature of human mind and consciousness is one of the perennial, deep questions in philosophy. Assuming that God exists, what happens to the question? In a God-created world, what would human mind be?

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>Are NDEs just anecdotes, or do we now have enough information to begin collecting data on these experiences? Physicist Àlex Gómez-Marín discusses. #shorts</article>

items.title: Nancey Murphy - Did God Create Evil?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Can God and the enormity of evil both exist? To theists, it’s the problem of evil, which they struggle to resolve. To atheists, it’s the argument from evil, which they wield like a sword. Theists try to absolve God by differentiating ‘God allowing evil’ from ‘God creating evil’. But if God is all-powerful and all-knowing, how could God not have created evil?

Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Nancey Murphy is an American philosopher and theologian who is Professor of Christian Philosophy at Fuller Theological Seminary, Pasadena, CA.

items.title: Galen Strawson - Dualism: Arguing Pro and Con
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Dualism is the theory that ultimate reality is composed of two radically different kinds of things, physical things and non-physical things, and that these same two kinds of things, expressed as body/brain and spirit/soul, account for consciousness. What are the arguments for and against Dualism (like the ‘interaction problem’)?

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Galen John Strawson is a British analytic philosopher and literary critic who works primarily on philosophy of mind, metaphysics, John Locke, David Hume, Immanuel Kant and Friedrich Nietzsche.
